body.title: Fix _gate_clear to parse YYYY-MM-DD date gates
body.result: fixed _gate_clear: date-first-token YYYY-MM-DD compared to today; fail-closed on unknown; 7 new tests green — ADC 85bb967, TheIgors 08688948
body.description:
cc_queue.py _gate_clear() only understands ticket-ID gates. When a gate string starts with a date (e.g. '2026-06-19 — 30 days after T-skill-telemetry-rollup...'), the function finds the ticket ID in the string, sees it is closed, and wrongly returns True — allowing the ticket to be picked up before its gate date. Fix: parse the first token; if it matches YYYY-MM-DD, compare to today and return False until that date passes. If neither a date nor a ticket ID is found, fail closed (return False). Add logging: '[gate-date] {id} blocked until {date}' when a date gate holds.

**Affected files:** ~/TheIgors/lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** _gate_clear only; no changes to any other gate logic or to the queue list display
**Test plan:** 3 new tests — future date blocks, past date clears, unknown-format fails closed; run existing cc_queue test suite green
**Validation:** cc_queue.py list --gated shows T-consequence-skill-telemetry hidden until 2026-06-19; log line '[gate-date]' appears when blocked
**Cleanup:** tests use tmp queue JSON fixture, no DB rows
**Logging:** log.debug('[gate-date] {ticket_id} blocked until {gate_date}') in _gate_clear when date gate holds
**Observable via:** log.debug('[gate-date] T-xxx blocked until 2026-06-19') in cc_queue.py:_gate_clear
